heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 19 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is no cold snap at the moment.  This year has experienced 6 cold snaps, with an average of 3.7 per year.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwaves right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 18 July 2025, making a total of only 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (202°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

run.status: The highest wind run ever seen is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 4.3°C), outside temperature (down 3.9°C), and feels like temperature (down 3.1°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.1 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in outside temperature (up 10.5°C), inside temperature (up 8.4°C), inside humidity (up 8%), and cloud levels (down 196 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 2.8m lower than yesterday, 195.8m higher than the past week, 221.6m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 623m minimum and 1932m maximum, with an average of 1253.2m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 1057.4m and 1031.7m.

summary.summary: Inside; cool or mild temperatures. Outside; extremely cold temperatures will prevail. Also, the air will feel dry and little or no wind. Possible light showers, cloud cover will be variable, overcast at times, and light levels will be low during the day. Low pressure may lead to stormy or rainy weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (4.2°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
